info.title: Pixel Lab API
info.description:
The API provides endpoints for creating AI-generated pixel art images, rotations,
animations, and more. Making it easy for applications to integrate pixel art
generation capabilities.

### Python Client

For convenience, a Python client library is available to simplify integration with your applications.
Visit our [GitHub repository](https://github.com/pixellab-code/pixellab-python) for installation 
instructions and examples.

```bash
pip install pixellab
```

### Authentication

The API uses a simple token based authentication system. After creating an account, you can find your API token 
in your [account settings](https://pixellab.ai/account). Include this token in all API requests using the Bearer authentication scheme:

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.pixellab.ai/v1/generate-image-pixflux \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "description": "cute dragon",
        "image_size": {"width": 128, "height": 128}
    }'
```

Or use the Python client:

```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")
client.generate_image_pixflux(
    description="cute dragon",
    image_size=dict(width=128, height=128),
)
```

### Supported models

- **Generate Image Bitforge**: Apply custom art styles using reference images.
- **Generate Image Pixflux**: Generate pixel art from text descriptions.
- **Animate (skeleton)**: Generate 4 frames of an animation from skeleton poses.
- **Animate (text)**: Generate animation from text descriptions.
- **Inpaint**: Edit and modify existing pixel art.
- **Rotate**: Rotate an object or a character.

post.description:
Creates a pixel art image based on the provided parameters. Called "Create image (new)" in the plugin.

Supported image size: 
- Minimum area 32x32 and maximum area 400x400  

Supported features:
- Init image
- Forced palette
- Transparent background

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.generate_image_pixflux(
    description="cute dragon",
    image_size=dict(width=128, height=128),
)
response.image.pil_image()
```

Generates a pixel art image based on the provided parameters. Called "Generate image (style)" in the plugin.

Supported image size: 
- Maximum area 200x200  

Supported features:
- Style image
- Inpainting
- Init image
- Forced palette
- Transparent background

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.generate_image_bitforge(
    description="cute dragon",
    image_size=dict(width=128, height=128),
)
response.image.pil_image()
```

Creates a pixel art animation based on the provided parameters. Called "Animate with skeleton" in the plugin.

Supported image sizes: 
- 16x16
- 32x32
- 64x64
- 128x128
- 256x256  

Supported features:
- Inpainting
- Init image
- Forced palette

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.animate_with_skeleton(
    view="side",
    direction="south",
    image_size=dict(width=64, height=64),
    reference_image=reference_image,
    inpainting_images=existing_animation_frames,
    mask_images=mask_images,
    skeleton_keypoints=skeleton_keypoints,
)
images = [image.pil_image() for image in response.images]
```

Creates a pixel art animation based on text description and parameters.

Supported image sizes: 
- 64x64

Supported features:
- Text-guided animation generation
- Inpainting
- Init image
- Forced palette
- Multiple frames

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.animate_with_text(
    description="human mage",
    action="walk",
    view="side",
    direction="south",
    image_size=dict(width=64, height=64),
    reference_image=reference_image,
    n_frames=4
)
images = [image.pil_image() for image in response.images]
```

Rotates a pixel art image based on the provided parameters. Called "Rotate" in the plugin.

Supported image sizes: 
- 16x16
- 32x32
- 64x64
- 128x128    

Supported features:
- Init image
- Forced palette

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.rotate(
    from_view="side",
    to_view="side",
    from_direction="south",
    to_direction="east",
    image_size=dict(width=16, height=16),
    from_image=image_of_subject_facing_south,
)
response.image.pil_image()
```

Creates a pixel art image based on the provided parameters. Called "Inpaint" in the plugin.

Supported image size: 
- Maximum area 200x200

Supported features:
- Inpainting
- Init image
- Forced palette
- Transparent background

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.inpaint(
    description="boy with wings",
    image_size=dict(width=16, height=16),
    inpainting_image=image_of_boy_without_wings,
    mask_image=mask_image,
)
response.image.pil_image()
```

Estimates the skeleton of a character, returning a list of keypoints to use with the skeleton animation tool.

Supported image sizes: 
- 16x16
- 32x32
- 64x64
- 128x128
- 256x256

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")

response = client.estimate_skeleton(
    image=image_of_the_character_on_a_transparent_background,
)
response.keypoints
```

get.description:
Returns the current balance for your account.

Using the Python client:
```python
import pixellab

client = pixellab.Client(secret="YOUR_API_TOKEN")
balance = client.get_balance()
print(f"Current balance: {balance.usd} USD")
```

guidance_scale.title: Guidance Scale
guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the reference image and skeleton keypoints

init_images.title: Init Images
init_images.description: Initial images to start the generation from

init_image_strength.title: Init Image Strength
init_image_strength.description: Strength of the initial image influence

inpainting_images.title: Inpainting Images
inpainting_images.description: Images used for showing the model with connected skeleton

mask_images.title: Mask Images
mask_images.description: Inpainting / mask image (black and white image, where the white is where the model should inpaint)

color_image.description: Forced color palette, image containing colors used for palette

AnimateWithSkeletonRequest.title: AnimateWithSkeletonRequest
AnimateWithSkeletonRequest.description: Request model for animation using skeleton endpoint

negative_description.title: Negative Description
negative_description.description: Negative prompt to guide what not to generate

text_guidance_scale.title: Text Guidance Scale
text_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the text prompts

image_guidance_scale.title: Image Guidance Scale
image_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the reference image

n_frames.title: N Frames
n_frames.description: Length of full animation (the model will always generate 4 frames)

start_frame_index.title: Start Frame Index
start_frame_index.description: Starting frame index of the full animation

init_images.title: Init Images
init_images.description: Initial images to start the generation from

init_image_strength.title: Init Image Strength
init_image_strength.description: Strength of the initial image influence

inpainting_images.title: Inpainting Images
inpainting_images.description: Existing animation frames to guide the generation

mask_images.title: Mask Images
mask_images.description: Inpainting / mask image (black and white image, where the white is where the model should inpaint)

color_image.description: Forced color palette, image containing colors used for palette

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Seed for reproducible results (0 for random)

AnimateWithTextRequest.title: AnimateWithTextRequest
AnimateWithTextRequest.description: Request model for animation using text endpoint

Base64Image.title: Base64Image
Base64Image.description:
A base64 encoded image.

Attributes:
    type (Literal["base64"]): Always "base64" to indicate the image encoding type
    base64 (str): The base64 encoded image data

CreditsResponse.title: CreditsResponse
CreditsResponse.description:
Response model for credits endpoint

Attributes:
    credits (float): The user's current credit balance

image.description: Image for which to estimate the skeleton

EstimateSkeletonRequest.title: EstimateSkeletonRequest
EstimateSkeletonRequest.description: Request model for estimate skeleton endpoint

text_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the text description

init_image_strength.description: Strength of the initial image influence

mask_image.description: Inpainting / mask image (black and white image, where the white is where the model should inpaint)

color_image.description: Forced color palette, image containing colors used for palette

skeleton_guidance_scale.title: Skeleton Guidance Scale
skeleton_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the skeleton keypoints

skeleton_keypoints.title: Skeleton Keypoints
skeleton_keypoints.description: Skeleton points. Warning! Sizes that are not 16x16, 32x32 and 64x64 can cause the generations to be lower quality

GenerateImageBitforgeRequest.description: Request model for image generation endpoint

description.title: Description

text_guidance_scale.title: Text Guidance Scale
text_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the text description

no_background.title: No Background
no_background.description: Generate with transparent background, (blank background over 200x200 area)

init_image_strength.title: Init Image Strength
init_image_strength.description: Strength of the initial image influence

color_image.description: Forced color palette, image containing colors used for palette

description.title: Description
description.description: Text description of the image to generate

negative_description.description: Text description of what to avoid in the generated image

text_guidance_scale.title: Text Guidance Scale
text_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the text description

InpaintRequest.description: Request model for image generation endpoint

image_guidance_scale.title: Image Guidance Scale
image_guidance_scale.description: How closely to follow the reference image

view_change.description: How many degrees to tilt the subject

direction_change.title: Direction Change

init_image_strength.description: Strength of the initial image influence

RotateRequest.description: Request model for image generation endpoint
